startup.canTakeover: ClawMaster can manage your existing OpenClaw installation
startup.canHelp: ClawMaster can help you install OpenClaw
startup.needNodejs: Please install Node.js and npm first
startup.tauriNotDetectedDesc: The app could not detect the Tauri desktop environment. Please reinstall from the official package.
dashboard.title: Overview
dashboard.costTrackingDesc: View token usage, API costs, and context health
dashboard.skillMarketDesc: Photo Q&A, invoice sorting — ready to use
dashboard.task.title: Recommended task flows
dashboard.task.subtitle: Use these guided entry points when you know the outcome you want but not the module path yet.
dashboard.task.feishu.title: Connect Feishu or Lark
dashboard.task.feishu.outcome: Bring a production messaging path online with the right runtime pieces and validation steps.
dashboard.task.feishu.step1: Confirm provider and default model are ready
dashboard.task.feishu.step2: Start the gateway before channel login
dashboard.task.feishu.step3: Install or verify required channel runtime pieces
dashboard.task.feishu.step4: Validate login and inspect logs if delivery fails
dashboard.task.cost.title: Control cost and usage
dashboard.task.cost.outcome: Check spend, token pressure, and model choices before waste spreads across the runtime.
dashboard.task.cost.step1: Review Observe data and recent runtime health
dashboard.task.cost.step2: Verify the active default model and provider
dashboard.task.cost.step3: Inspect recent sessions when context pressure rises
dashboard.task.cost.step4: Use diagnostics when usage data looks inconsistent
dashboard.task.private.title: Operate a private deployment
dashboard.task.private.outcome: Keep gateway exposure, auth, and profile isolation under control in a self-managed environment.
dashboard.task.private.step1: Check which OpenClaw profile is active
dashboard.task.private.step2: Verify gateway bind and auth settings
dashboard.task.private.step3: Use raw config only for the final low-level edits
dashboard.task.private.step4: Review diagnostics when runtime state drifts from UI
dashboard.task.extend.title: Extend assistant capabilities
dashboard.task.extend.outcome: Add tools and runtime extensions without losing track of what is actually enabled.
dashboard.task.extend.step1: Add or import MCP servers first
dashboard.task.extend.step2: Enable only the plugins you need
dashboard.task.extend.step3: Install skills that match the workflow
dashboard.task.extend.step4: Verify runtime state after every enable or install
gateway.title: Gateway Management
gateway.editConfigHint: Edit config in the Config page
config.title: Config Management
config.advancedHint: Providers and channels are managed in their pages. This page is for advanced JSON editing.
models.title: Model Configuration
models.textProvidersDesc: Primary chat and reasoning providers for agents, tools, and workspace defaults.
models.imageProvidersDesc: Standalone text-to-image providers for image defaults and media workflows.
models.noProviders: No providers configured. Click "+ Add Provider" above to start
models.ernieImageSkillInstallNotice: ClawMaster will install and enable the bundled ERNIE-Image Guide skill when you add this provider, so users get ERNIE-specific prompt and parameter guidance automatically.
providers.ernieQuotaNote: Get 1,000,000 free tokens after registration, then another 1,000,000 after completing your profile.
providers.ernieImageNote: Uses the same Baidu AI Studio Access Token. Verification performs a real image generation request.
providers.ernieImageGuide: Use this provider for image generation, not as the primary agent chat model.
providers.ernieImageSkillDesc: Use this ERNIE-specific skill when you want help shaping prompts and choosing Baidu image arguments such as size, seed, use_pe, steps, and guidance scale.
providers.googleImageGuide: Use Gemini Image for image generation tasks. Keep Gemini chat models in the text provider section.
providers.gptImageGuide: Use GPT Image for image generation tasks. Keep GPT chat and reasoning models in the text provider section.
providers.imageGenerateSkillDesc: Use this workflow skill when you want help shaping prompts, choosing aspect ratios, and tuning provider-specific image settings.
channels.title: Channel Management
skills.title: Skill Market
skills.photoQaDesc: Photo → OCR → AI solve → answer, use via Feishu/WeChat/DingTalk
skills.subtitle: Install from ClawHub, then enable only the skills your OpenClaw runtime should expose
skills.clawhub.title: Install ClawHub CLI first
skills.clawhub.missing: ClawHub CLI was not found on this machine.
skills.clawhub.description: Marketplace installs and updates depend on the ClawHub CLI. Install it here first so skill actions work reliably.
skills.featuredLead: These featured skills cover bundled provider guidance and proven marketplace workflows without forcing users through a huge catalog first.
skills.runtimeLead: Installed skills are live inventory. Toggling here writes the real OpenClaw skills config.
skills.liveConfigHint: ClawMaster writes skills.entries.<skillKey>.enabled. OpenClaw picks up the change on the next session or on the next turn after skills reload.
skills.scenesLead: Use bundles when you want a ready-made workflow instead of assembling individual skills one by one.
skills.installedLead: Review what OpenClaw can currently expose, then disable anything you do not want in prompt scope.
skills.curatedLead: Browse the curated shelf first, then use live ClawHub search only when you need something more specific.
skills.scanSubtitle: Static security review for this installed skill
skills.scanMoreFindings: +{{count}} more findings in the full report
skills.scanDialogLead: Open the full audit when you need to inspect the path, severity mix, and remediation notes before keeping a skill enabled.
skills.slugMismatchTitle: Runtime name differs from install slug
skills.slugMismatchHint: Installs as {{slug}} but OpenClaw exposes it as {{key}}.
skills.collisionHint: Another installed skill already uses {{key}} from {{source}}. Verify the source before enabling or troubleshooting.
skills.codingBoostDesc: Self-improvement + quality coding practices for evolving AI assistants
skills.catalog.paddleocrDoc.desc: PaddleOCR-based document parsing for scanned files, PDFs, and complex layouts
skills.catalog.paddleocrText.desc: PaddleOCR image text recognition supporting multilingual handwriting and print
skills.catalog.writingStyle.desc: Auto-learns your writing style from corrections and continuously improves output
skills.catalog.selfImproving.desc: Captures learnings, errors, and improvements across sessions for continuous AI evolution
skills.catalog.agencyPack.desc: 80+ specialist AI agents covering engineering, design, marketing, testing, and more
skills.catalog.okSkills.desc: Curated AI coding agent skills and AGENTS.md playbooks, multi-tool compatible
skills.catalog.memoryPro.desc: Hierarchical memory system: memory to knowledge to skill, auto-distills knowledge
skills.catalog.skillnote.desc: Open-source skill registry for distributing and managing SKILL.md files across platforms
skills.catalog.ernieImage.desc: ERNIE-Image-specific guide for prompt shaping and choosing Baidu image arguments like size, seed, use_pe, steps, and guidance scale
skills.catalog.imageGenerate.desc: Guided image generation workflow skill for turning goals into prompts and runnable image tasks
skills.catalog.clawvet.desc: Skill security vetting tool that scans for injection attacks and supply chain risks
skills.catalog.ontoskills.desc: OWL 2 skill compiler that transforms skills into validated semantic web ontologies
skills.catalog.findSkills.desc: Skill discovery guide for searching skills from ClawHub, GitHub, and other sources
settings.title: Settings
settings.dangerWarning: These actions are irreversible. Proceed with caution.
settings.resetConfigConfirm: Are you sure you want to reset the config to defaults? This cannot be undone.
settings.uninstallConfirm: Are you sure you want to uninstall OpenClaw? This cannot be undone.
settings.aboutDesc: The hexagonal warrior of the OpenClaw ecosystem
logs.title: Logs
logs.gatewayDescription: Use this when the gateway fails to start, restart, bind, or authenticate correctly.
logs.channelsDescription: Check recent logs when channel verification, login, or account setup fails.
logs.settingsDescription: Open recent system logs here when you need to troubleshoot runtime issues.
logs.noScopedLogs: No related log lines found in the last {{count}} lines.
docs.title: Documentation
docs.subtitle: Local-first help for setup, operations, and troubleshooting.
docs.heroTitle: Find the next step without leaving ClawMaster
docs.heroDesc: Start with bundled guides, troubleshooting notes, and copyable commands. Use live docs search only when you need deeper upstream reference.
docs.searchHint: Search the built-in help index instantly, then fall back to live docs when needed.
docs.searchMode.local.title: Local-first reference
docs.searchMode.local.desc: Bundled guides and troubleshooting cards load immediately and stay useful even when upstream search is unavailable.
docs.searchMode.context.title: Task to module mapping
docs.searchMode.context.desc: Each card points back to the exact ClawMaster page where the operational work actually happens.
docs.searchMode.live.title: Upstream fallback
docs.searchMode.live.desc: Live search runs `openclaw docs <query>` for deeper reference when the built-in index is not enough.
docs.localResultsDesc: {{count}} local matches across tasks, guides, commands, and troubleshooting.
docs.noLocalResultsDesc: Try a broader keyword or use live docs search for upstream results.
docs.liveSearchHelp: Live search uses `openclaw docs <query>` and depends on the local OpenClaw CLI/runtime.
docs.liveSearchEmpty: Live docs search returned no structured results.
docs.section.scenariosDesc: Start from goals instead of internal module names.
docs.section.guidesDesc: Open the official references users need most often.
docs.section.commandsDesc: Practical CLI snippets already reflected in ClawMaster workflows.
docs.section.troubleshootingDesc: Fast paths for the issues users hit most often.
docs.quickStartDesc: Install, configure, send your first message
docs.modelConfigDesc: Provider API Keys, model selection, fallback chain
docs.scenario.feishu.title: Connect Feishu or Lark
docs.scenario.feishu.desc: Use the channel flow, install required runtime pieces, and verify the account path.
docs.scenario.cost.title: Control cost and usage
docs.scenario.cost.desc: Review spend and token trends, inspect runtime diagnostics, and tune the active model stack.
docs.scenario.private.title: Operate a private deployment
docs.scenario.private.desc: Check gateway bind and auth settings, start services safely, and keep profile isolation clear.
docs.scenario.extend.title: Extend the assistant
docs.scenario.extend.desc: Add MCP servers, plugins, and skills without losing track of what is enabled in the runtime.
docs.troubleshooting.gateway.title: Gateway will not start
docs.troubleshooting.gateway.desc: Check port conflicts, auth config, and recent gateway diagnostics before editing raw config.
docs.troubleshooting.channel.title: Channel login or webhook setup fails
docs.troubleshooting.channel.desc: Open channel logs first, then review provider credentials and required plugin state.
docs.troubleshooting.model.title: Model calls fail or the fallback chain looks wrong
docs.troubleshooting.model.desc: Review provider API keys, base URLs, and the active default model from the Models page.
docs.troubleshooting.runtime.title: Runtime state does not match the UI
docs.troubleshooting.runtime.desc: Check the selected OpenClaw profile in Settings, then verify plugins, skills, and MCP against that profile.
docs.command.gatewayStart.title: Start the gateway
docs.command.gatewayStart.desc: Start the gateway from the CLI.
docs.command.docsSearch.title: Search official docs from the CLI
docs.command.docsSearch.desc: Use the local OpenClaw CLI when you need deeper upstream reference.
docs.command.modelSet.title: Set the default model quickly
docs.command.modelSet.desc: Change the active default model after configuring a provider.
docs.command.pluginsList.title: Inspect plugin runtime state
docs.command.pluginsList.desc: Read plugin status in machine-readable JSON when the runtime and UI differ.
setup.appSlogan: The hexagonal warrior of the OpenClaw ecosystem
setup.coreReadyOptional: Core ready! {{count}} optional extensions can be installed later
setup.optionalLater: Uninstalled capabilities can be installed from their feature pages
setup.apiKeyInvalid: API Key invalid or network unreachable. Please check and retry.
ollama.installDesc: Ollama runs LLMs locally on your machine. Click below to install automatically.
ollama.noModels: No models pulled yet. Pull a model to get started.
setup.gatewayTimeout: Gateway start timed out. Check if port is in use.
setup.channelOptional: Optional, can be added later in Channels
observe.title: Observe
observe.ensureProbe: Ensure ClawProbe is installed and running (clawprobe start)
observe.unavailable: Observability requires ClawProbe. After installation, you can view token usage, API costs, and context health.
memory.title: Memory Management
memory.unavailable: Memory management requires PowerMem. After install, manage agent long-term memory with 96% token reduction.
memory.ensureInstalled: Ensure PowerMem is installed and running (powermem-server --port 8000)
memory.inferHint: Content will be AI-extracted for key facts before storage (infer mode)
channel.discord.tokenHint: Get from Bot page → Reset Token, ~70 chars
channel.slack.botTokenHint: Starts with xoxb-, from OAuth & Permissions page
channel.slack.appTokenHint: Starts with xapp-, from Basic Information → App-Level Tokens
channel.feishu.name: Feishu (Lark)
channel.feishu.tokenHint: Get App ID from Credentials page
channel.feishu.step1.highlight: Open Platform → Create Custom App → Name & Description → Confirm
channel.feishu.step2.highlight: Credentials & Basic Info → Copy App ID and App Secret
channel.feishu.step6.highlight: Version Management → Create Version → Submit → Admin Approval
capability.defaultUnavailable: This feature requires {{name}}. Click below to install.
channel.wechat.name: WeChat
channel.wechat.step2.highlight: Scan QR code with WeChat on phone → Confirm
channel.wechat.step3.highlight: Messages auto-received and replied after login
channel.whatsapp.step1.highlight: ClawMaster will generate a QR code
channel.whatsapp.step2.highlight: Phone WhatsApp → Settings → Linked Devices → Scan QR code
channel.whatsapp.step3.highlight: Messages auto-received and replied after login
channel.qr.desc: This channel uses phone QR scan login — no manual token needed
channel.qr.scanHint: Scan the QR code with your phone app to log in

channel.wechat.step1.highlight: Automatically installed when you click Start

observe.errorLi2: Run clawprobe start to begin monitoring

channelsPage.title: Channels
channelsPage.subtitle: Start with the inbox your team already uses, then expand only when you need more coverage.
channelsPage.recommendedHeadline: Start with the fastest path to a real inbox
channelsPage.recommendedDesc: Most teams only need one or two channels. Prioritize Feishu or WeChat for China-facing workflows, and Discord or Slack for engineering teams and communities.
channelsPage.quickStepChooseDesc: Pick the entry point your users already live in instead of configuring every connector up front.
channelsPage.quickStepVerifyDesc: Use the guided fields, then validate the connection before you save the config.
channelsPage.quickStepBindDesc: Route new conversations to the right agent as part of the same setup flow.
channelsPage.focusDesc: These channels are surfaced first because they are the most likely production starting points.
channelsPage.group.chinaDesc: Best for internal assistants, staff workflows, enterprise approvals, and customer operations in China.
channelsPage.group.workspaceDesc: Best for engineering teams, workspace automation, community bots, and multi-server support.
channelsPage.hint.feishu: Fastest path for enterprise assistants and approval-driven workflows.
channelsPage.hint.wechat: Best when you need WeCom or WeChat-facing conversations close to users.
channelsPage.hint.discord: Best for community servers, support channels, and internal Discord ops.
channelsPage.hint.slack: Best for engineering workspaces, alerts, and team-facing automation.
channelsPage.configuredSectionDesc: Review live channels, manage account states, or jump back into channel-specific settings.
channelsPage.secondaryDesc: Use these when the recommended channels are not the right fit for the team.
channelsPage.footerNote: Field hints match the setup wizard; enable/disable toggles config.
channelsPage.modalAddBody: Opens guided editor for this channel type.

memory.pmemNotConfigured: PowerMem not configured. Enable the memory-powermem plugin first.
memory.pluginMissing: Memory plugin not found. Install it via Plugins page.
